# invr

> Declarative data validation engine for Rust. Define invariants (validation rules) and evaluate them against datasets using a typed execution engine.

- **Rust crate**: `invr` on crates.io — `cargo add invr --features polars,yaml`
- **Python package**: `invr` on PyPI — `pip install invr`
- **Repository**: https://github.com/lsalenson/invr
- **Docs (Rust)**: https://docs.rs/invr
- **License**: Apache-2.0

---

## Core Concepts

- **Invariant** — A typed validation rule (e.g. "column must not be null"). Composed of: `id`, `kind`, `scope`, `severity`, `params`.
- **Spec** — An ordered, deduplicated collection of `Invariant`s.
- **Scope** — Where the rule applies: `Dataset` (whole dataframe) or `Column { name }` (one column).
- **Severity** — `Error` (default) | `Warn` | `Info`. Only `Error` violations cause `report.failed()` to return `true`.
- **Engine** — Executes a `Spec` against data. Default: `EnginePolarsDataFrame` (Polars lazy execution).
- **Report** — Result of running a spec. Contains `Violation`s with reasons and optional metrics/examples.
- **Violation** — A failed invariant. Has `invariant_id`, `severity`, `scope`, `reason`, optional `metrics`, optional `examples`.

### Execution model
```
Invariant
   ↓ plan()  →  Polars Expr (metric expression, lazily evaluated)
   ↓ execute()  →  scalar metric value
   ↓ map()  →  Option<Violation>
```

---

## Installation

### Rust
```toml
# Cargo.toml
[dependencies]
invars = { version = "0.1", features = ["polars"] }
# With YAML loading:
invars = { version = "0.1", features = ["polars", "yaml"] }
```

### Python
```bash
pip install invars
# Requires: pandas >= 1.5, pyarrow >= 10.0, Python >= 3.9
```

---

## Quick Start — Rust (programmatic spec)

```rust
use invr::prelude::*;
use polars::prelude::*;

let df = df![
    "age"   => [25, 30, 45],
    "email" => ["a@b.com", "c@d.com", "e@f.com"],
]?;

let spec = Spec::from_invariants(vec![
    Invariant::new(
        InvariantId::new("age_not_null")?,
        PolarsKind::NotNull,
        Scope::column("age"),
    ),
    Invariant::new(
        InvariantId::new("row_min")?,
        PolarsKind::RowCountMin,
        Scope::Dataset,
    )
    .with_param_value("min", "1"),
    Invariant::new(
        InvariantId::new("email_unique")?,
        PolarsKind::Unique,
        Scope::column("email"),
    )
    .with_severity(Severity::Warn),
]);

let report = RunSpec::new(EnginePolarsDataFrame).run(&df, &spec)?;

if report.failed() {
    for v in report.errors() {
        eprintln!("[{}] {} — {}", v.invariant_id().as_str(), v.scope(), v.reason());
    }
}
```

## Quick Start — Rust (YAML spec)

```rust
use invr::prelude::*;

let yaml = std::fs::read_to_string("spec.yaml")?;
let spec = spec_from_str(&yaml)?;

let runner = RunSpec::new(EnginePolarsDataFrame);
let report = runner.run(&df, &spec)?;
```

## Quick Start — Python

```python
import pandas as pd
from invars import run

spec_yaml = """
invariants:
  - id: age_not_null
    kind: not_null
    scope:
      type: column
      name: age
  - id: row_min
    kind: row_count_min
    scope:
      type: dataset
    params:
      min: "10"
"""

df = pd.DataFrame({"age": [25, None, 45]})
report = run(spec_yaml, df)

if report.failed():
    for v in report.violations:
        print(v.invariant_id, v.severity, v.reason)
```

## Quick Start — CLI

```bash
invars run --spec spec.yaml --data data.parquet
invars run --spec spec.yaml --data data.csv --format json
invars run --spec spec.yaml --data data.parquet --fail-on-violations false
```

---

## YAML Spec Format

```yaml
invariants:
  - id: <string>              # unique identifier, [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+
    kind: <kind>              # snake_case kind name (see reference below)
    scope:
      type: dataset           # dataset-level invariant
      # OR
      type: column
      name: <column_name>     # column-level invariant
    severity: error           # error (default) | warn | info
    params:                   # optional; all values are strings
      key: "value"
```

---

## Invariant Reference (all 33 kinds)

All param values are strings. `row_count_cache` is injected automatically by the engine — do not set it manually.

### Nullability

| YAML kind | Rust variant | Scope | Required params | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `not_null` | `NotNull` | Column | — | Fails if any null exists |
| `null_ratio_max` | `NullRatioMax` | Column | `max_ratio` | Float 0.0–1.0. Fails if null% > max_ratio |

### Uniqueness

| YAML kind | Rust variant | Scope | Required params | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `unique` | `Unique` | Column | — | All values must be distinct |
| `composite_unique` | `CompositeUnique` | Dataset | `columns` | Comma-sep column list, e.g. `"a,b"` |
| `duplicate_ratio_max` | `DuplicateRatioMax` | Column | `max_ratio` | Float 0.0–1.0 |

### Row Count

| YAML kind | Rust variant | Scope | Required params |
|---|---|---|---|
| `row_count_min` | `RowCountMin` | Dataset | `min` (integer) |
| `row_count_max` | `RowCountMax` | Dataset | `max` (integer) |
| `row_count_between` | `RowCountBetween` | Dataset | `min`, `max` (integers) |

### Structure

| YAML kind | Rust variant | Scope | Required params | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `column_exists` | `ColumnExists` | Column | — | Column must be present in schema |
| `column_missing` | `ColumnMissing` | Column | — | Column must NOT be present |
| `dtype_is` | `DTypeIs` | Column | `dtype` | Polars dtype string, e.g. `"Int64"`, `"Utf8"`, `"Float64"` |
| `schema_equals` | `SchemaEquals` | Dataset | `schema` | Comma-sep `col:dtype` pairs, e.g. `"a:Int64,b:Utf8"` |

### Numeric

| YAML kind | Rust variant | Scope | Required params |
|---|---|---|---|
| `value_min` | `ValueMin` | Column | `min` (numeric) |
| `value_max` | `ValueMax` | Column | `max` (numeric) |
| `value_between` | `ValueBetween` | Column | `min`, `max` (numeric) |
| `mean_between` | `MeanBetween` | Column | `min`, `max` (numeric) |
| `stddev_max` | `StdDevMax` | Column | `max` (numeric) |
| `sum_between` | `SumBetween` | Column | `min`, `max` (numeric) |

### Date / Time

| YAML kind | Rust variant | Scope | Required params | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `date_between` | `DateBetween` | Column | `start`, `end` | ISO 8601 date strings |
| `no_future_dates` | `NoFutureDates` | Column | — | No value after today |
| `monotonic_increasing` | `MonotonicIncreasing` | Column | — | Each value ≥ previous |
| `no_gaps_in_sequence` | `NoGapsInSequence` | Column | — | No missing integers in sequence |

### String

| YAML kind | Rust variant | Scope | Required params |
|---|---|---|---|
| `regex_match` | `RegexMatch` | Column | `pattern` (regex string) |
| `string_length_min` | `StringLengthMin` | Column | `min` (integer) |
| `string_length_max` | `StringLengthMax` | Column | `max` (integer) |
| `string_length_between` | `StringLengthBetween` | Column | `min`, `max` (integers) |

### Domain

| YAML kind | Rust variant | Scope | Required params | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `allowed_values` | `AllowedValues` | Column | `values` | Comma-sep string list, e.g. `"A,B,C"` |
| `forbidden_values` | `ForbiddenValues` | Column | `values` | Comma-sep string list |

### Statistical

| YAML kind | Rust variant | Scope | Required params | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `outlier_ratio_max` | `OutlierRatioMax` | Column | `z`, `max_ratio` | `z` = Z-score threshold (float), `max_ratio` = float 0.0–1.0 |
| `percentile_between` | `PercentileBetween` | Column | `p`, `min`, `max` | `p` = percentile 0.0–1.0, `min`/`max` = expected bounds |

### Relational

| YAML kind | Rust variant | Scope | Required params | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `foreign_key` | `ForeignKey` | Column | `allowed_values` | Comma-sep list of valid foreign key values |
| `column_equals` | `ColumnEquals` | Column | `other_column` | Column name to compare against |
| `conditional_not_null` | `ConditionalNotNull` | Column | `condition_column`, `condition_value` | Not null when `condition_column == condition_value` |

### Custom

| YAML kind | Rust variant | Scope | Required params | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `custom_expr` | `CustomExpr` | Column | `column` | Column name; write custom Polars expr logic directly |

---

## Rust API Reference

### `Invariant<K>`

```rust
// Construction
Invariant::new(id: InvariantId, kind: K, scope: Scope) -> Self

// Builder methods
.with_severity(Severity) -> Self
.with_param_value(key: impl Into<String>, value: impl Into<String>) -> Self
.with_params(BTreeMap<String, String>) -> Self

// Accessors
.id() -> &InvariantId
.kind() -> &K
.scope() -> &Scope
.severity() -> Severity
.param(key) -> Option<&str>
.require_param(key) -> Result<&str, _>
.has_param(key) -> bool
```

### `InvariantId`

```rust
InvariantId::new(value: &str) -> Result<Self, _>  // [a-zA-Z0-9_-] only
.as_str() -> &str
```

### `Scope`

```rust
Scope::Dataset
Scope::Column { name: String }
Scope::dataset() -> Self
Scope::column(name: impl Into<String>) -> Self
```

### `Severity`

```rust
Severity::Error  // default
Severity::Warn
Severity::Info
.is_error() -> bool
.is_warn() -> bool
.is_info() -> bool
.level() -> u8   // Error=2, Warn=1, Info=0
```

### `Spec<K>`

```rust
Spec::new() -> Self
Spec::from_invariants(vec: Vec<Invariant<K>>) -> Self
.validate() -> Result<(), _>         // check no duplicate IDs
.push(invariant) -> &mut Self
.extend(iter) -> &mut Self
.invariants() -> &[Invariant<K>]
.find_by_id(id: &InvariantId) -> Option<&Invariant<K>>
.find_by_str(id: &str) -> Option<&Invariant<K>>
.len() -> usize
.is_empty() -> bool
.iter() -> impl Iterator<Item = &Invariant<K>>
```

### `RunSpec<E>`

```rust
RunSpec::new(engine: E) -> Self
.run(dataset: &E::Dataset, spec: &Spec<E::Kind>) -> ApplicationResult<Report>
```

### `Report`

```rust
.failed() -> bool               // true if any Error violation
.has_errors() -> bool
.has_warnings() -> bool
.violations() -> &[Violation]
.errors() -> impl Iterator<Item = &Violation>
.warnings() -> impl Iterator<Item = &Violation>
.error_count() -> usize
.warn_count() -> usize
.info_count() -> usize
.metrics() -> &ReportMetrics     // { total_invariants, violations, execution_time_ms }
.len() -> usize
.is_empty() -> bool
.iter() -> impl Iterator<Item = &Violation>
```

### `Violation`

```rust
.invariant_id() -> &InvariantId
.severity() -> Severity
.scope() -> &Scope
.reason() -> &str
.metrics() -> &[Metric]          // e.g. { name: "null_count", value: 3 }
.examples() -> &[String]
.has_metrics() -> bool
.has_examples() -> bool
```

### YAML loading (`yaml` feature)

```rust
use invr::interface::yaml::spec_from_str;
use invr::interface::yaml::load_spec_from_path;

spec_from_str(yaml: &str) -> Result<Spec<PolarsKind>, YamlError>
load_spec_from_path(path: &Path) -> Result<Spec<PolarsKind>, YamlError>
```

### Engine trait

```rust
pub trait Engine {
    type Dataset;
    type Kind;
    fn execute(&self, dataset: &Self::Dataset, spec: &Spec<Self::Kind>)
        -> ApplicationResult<Report>;
}
```

---

## Python API Reference

```python
from invars import run, run_file, Report, Violation

# Run a YAML spec string against a pandas DataFrame
run(spec: str, df: pd.DataFrame) -> Report

# Run a YAML spec file against a CSV / Parquet / IPC file path
run_file(spec_path: str, data_path: str) -> Report
```

### `Report`

```python
report.total_invariants   # int
report.violations_count   # int
report.execution_time_ms  # int
report.violations         # list[Violation]
report.passed() -> bool
report.failed() -> bool
report.to_dict() -> dict
```

### `Violation`

```python
v.invariant_id  # str
v.severity      # str: "error" | "warn" | "info"
v.reason        # str
v.column        # str | None
v.to_dict() -> dict
```

---

## CLI Reference

```
invars run [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
  --spec <PATH>                Path to YAML spec file
  --data <PATH>                Path to data file (CSV, Parquet, IPC/Arrow)
  --format <text|json>         Output format (default: text)
  --fail-on-violations <bool>  Exit code 1 on violations (default: true)
```

---

## Architecture

```
crates/
├── invars/            # Core library
│   └── src/
│       ├── domain/           # Invariant, Spec, Report, Violation, Scope, Severity
│       ├── application/      # RunSpec use case, Engine trait
│       ├── infrastructure/   # EnginePolarsDataFrame + 33 check implementations
│       └── interface/        # YAML loader (DTOs + mapper)
├── invars-cli/        # CLI binary (clap + text/json output)
└── invars-py/         # Python bindings via PyO3 + maturin
```

The core domain is engine-agnostic. To implement a custom engine:

```rust
use invr::application::engine::Engine;

struct MyEngine;

impl Engine for MyEngine {
    type Dataset = MyData;
    type Kind = PolarsKind;

    fn execute(&self, dataset: &MyData, spec: &Spec<PolarsKind>)
        -> ApplicationResult<Report> {
        // custom execution logic
    }
}
```
